reasoning: Paul Biedermann's 200m long course record (1:42.00) was set in 2009 during the supersuit era and is widely considered one of the hardest records to break. Since 2009, only David Popovici has broken the 1:43 mark (1:42.97 in 2022), which remains nearly a full second behind Biedermann's time. The winter months from November to January are traditionally dominated by short course (25m) swimming, meaning elite swimmers are highly unlikely to peak for a long course record attempt during this timeframe. Furthermore, world-class 200m sprint times in 2024 and 2025 have not approached the 1:42 barrier. The small remaining uncertainty predominantly accounts for baseline structural risks such as Wikipedia vandalism temporarily altering the page on the resolution date or bizarre retrospective record invalidations. Given the rules capping probability at 0.95 and prescribing increasing uncertainty over time, the estimates reflect a near-certainty mathematically slightly softened as the horizon extends.
